title: mangifera quadrifida var. longipetiolata
intro: Mangifera quadrifida var. longipetiolata, also known as the four-angled mango, is a variety of the mango plant native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the Mangifera quadrifida var. longipetiolata plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
